Pericallis pudica is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its unique habit of folding its leaves and flowers when disturbed. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Pericallis pudica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pericallis pudica is often used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine in some parts of the world.
